name: Tomato and Corn Chowder (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pound baking potatoes, peeled and cut into half-inch dice
4 medium-size ripe tomatoes, peeled and seeded, chopped and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large saucepan three-quarters full of salted water to a boil. Add the potatoes and cook until tender, about 10 minutes. Drain and reserve.
2. Melt butter in a large soup pot over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft, about 7 minutes.
3. Cut kernels from corn cobs and scrape cobs to release liquid; set aside juice and 1 cup kernels. Add remaining corn, chicken broth, and 4 cups water to the soup pot. Bring to a boil and simmer until the liquid is reduced by one-quarter, 15 minutes.
4. In a blender, puree the mixture in batches on high speed until smooth, about 2 to 3 minutes per batch. Strain through a fine sieve into a clean soup pan.
5. Add reserved corn, potatoes, and cream if desired. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Warm over medium-high heat just until hot, 3 to 4 minutes.
6. Add tomatoes and cook for 1 minute. Garnish with chives. Serve immediately. Serves 6.
